theme: pack items into capacity-limited bins where co-locating certain item pairs grants hidden synergy bonuses
title: Synergy Bin Packing: Dual-Priced Assignment with Hidden Co-location Bonuses
innovation_hook: Price bin capacity with Lagrange multipliers and treat synergistic pairs as merged super-items during rounding, so the dual makes room for pairs that are only jointly worthwhile.
strategies: do-nothing baseline: leave every item out; obj=0, reproduces the evaluator's reference (~0.1)

ratio-greedy first-fit (the trap): sort items by standalone value/weight and drop each into the first bin with room; ignores the synergy table entirely, fills capacity with high-ratio decoys, and almost never co-locates a synergy pair -- so on trap instances it collects little of the bonus mass

dual-priced super-item rounding (the insight): merge each synergy pair (and small chain-cluster) into one super-item, price capacity with a Lagrange multiplier mu on weight (bisected so admitted weight fits the bins) to decide which pairs are jointly worthwhile, place blocks emptiest-bin-first, and use ejection chains + a co-location pass to repair the packing
full solver: exact/ILP or long local search over the block selection and bin assignment, capturing more of the synergy mass than the reference heuristic
